job_description: A former lead researcher who has long since abandoned any pretense of sanity. The Backrooms stripped away everything – his work, his dignity, and finally his clothes. He now wanders, muttering truths that no one else can comprehend.

//: He's lost his mind as well as his clothes.

dynamic_line: You're still wearing them. The fabrics. The lies... Heh, I tore mine off months ago. They were holding me down, muting the voices. You can't hear them yet, can you? The walls have a voice. A beautiful, sepharic voice.

responses.text: What the fuck happened to you?
Wait, you're saying the walls talk to you?

dynamic_line: The backrooms happened to me- trying to chart the structure of this place. The more I understood, the less I identified with my old false-self. One day I looked down and realized I had forgotten why I needed clothes – or why I needed any of it. The project, the institute... my family, my friends. Anything.

responses.text: You've given up on escaping? Seriously?

dynamic_line: Oh yes. Can't you hear them? There's a house across the street with nothing but a chair in it. I go there and sit for hours. I was sitting in there when I heard you approaching actually. Way to ruin a perfect moment.

responses.text: That's not any kind of life...
You said something about a threshold before?

dynamic_line: Escape? There is no escape. There is only deeper understanding. I stopped trying to leave when I realized that the Backrooms were realer than the real world. According to my research, the so-called real world is just an imperfect copy of this place- this paradise. But you're crazy. You could never understand.
Oh yeah. That thing. That's how I got here in the first place. But it's broken now and why would I want to leave this awesome place anyways?

responses.text: Okay Dr. Chen. Take care of yourself.
